quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Synthesis reaction"?

options.text: Increasing pressure (decreasing volume) shifts equilibrium toward the side with fewer moles of gas; inert gas at constant V has no effect
Ka1 >> Ka2 >> Ka3; each successive proton is harder to remove because it leaves a more negatively charged anion
Two or more reactants combine to form one product: A + B → AB (e.g., 2Na + Cl₂ → 2NaCl)
Equilibrium involving species in more than one phase; pure solids and liquids have activity = 1 and are excluded from K

quiz_questions.explanation: "Synthesis reaction" is defined as: Two or more reactants combine to form one product: A + B → AB (e.g., 2Na + Cl₂ → 2NaCl). The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Decomposition reaction"?

options.text: The ratio of product to reactant concentrations at equilibrium, each raised to their stoichiometric coefficients; temperature-dependent
The rate at a specific moment, determined by the slope of the tangent to the concentration-vs.-time curve
One compound breaks down into two or more simpler substances: AB → A + B (e.g., 2H₂O₂ → 2H₂O + O₂)
A proton (H⁺) acceptor

quiz_questions.explanation: "Decomposition reaction" is defined as: One compound breaks down into two or more simpler substances: AB → A + B (e.g., 2H₂O₂ → 2H₂O + O₂). The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Oxidation-reduction (redox)"?

options.text: Reactions involving electron transfer; oxidation = loss of electrons (OIL), reduction = gain of electrons (RIG)
The arrangement of only the atoms (bonding pairs) around the central atom (e.g., bent for 2 bonding + 2 lone pairs)
The rate at a specific moment, determined by the slope of the tangent to the concentration-vs.-time curve
A model stating reactions occur when molecules collide with sufficient energy (≥ Ea) and proper orientation

quiz_questions.explanation: "Oxidation-reduction (redox)" is defined as: Reactions involving electron transfer; oxidation = loss of electrons (OIL), reduction = gain of electrons (RIG). The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Oxidation state"?

options.text: A substance that increases reaction rate by providing an alternative pathway with lower Ea; regenerated and not consumed
-log[H⁺]; a logarithmic scale where each unit represents a 10-fold change in [H⁺]; pH 3 is 1000× more acidic than pH 6

A weak acid or base that changes color at a specific pH range; chosen so its color change range includes the equivalence point pH (e.g., phenolphthalein, pH 8.2–10, ideal for weak acid–strong base titrations; bromothymol blue, pH 6.0–7.6, for...
The apparent charge on an atom in a compound, determined by electronegativity rules; tracks electron transfer in redox reactions

quiz_questions.explanation: "Oxidation state" is defined as: The apparent charge on an atom in a compound, determined by electronegativity rules; tracks electron transfer in redox reactions. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following best defines "Activity series"?

options.text: A model stating reactions occur when molecules collide with sufficient energy (≥ Ea) and proper orientation
A strong dipole-dipole interaction between H bonded to N, O, or F and a lone pair on another N, O, or F atom
A ranking of metals by reactivity (tendency to lose electrons); a more active metal displaces a less active metal from solution
The attraction between an ion and a polar molecule; the dominant force when ionic compounds dissolve in water

quiz_questions.explanation: "Activity series" is defined as: A ranking of metals by reactivity (tendency to lose electrons); a more active metal displaces a less active metal from solution. The other options describe different concepts.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "Two or more reactants combine to form one product: A + B → AB (e.g., 2Na + Cl₂ → 2NaCl)"?

options.text: ΔG° = -nFE°
Synthesis reaction
Standard reduction potential (E°)
ΔS prediction rules

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Synthesis reaction".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "One compound breaks down into two or more simpler substances: AB → A + B (e.g., 2H₂O₂ → 2H₂O + O₂)"?

options.text: Zwitterion
Decomposition reaction
Heat of vaporization
State function

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Decomposition reaction".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "Reactions involving electron transfer; oxidation = loss of electrons (OIL), reduction = gain of electrons (RIG)"?

options.text: Oxidation-reduction (redox)
Atomic number (Z)
Gibbs free energy (ΔG)
First law of thermodynamics

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Oxidation-reduction (redox)".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "The apparent charge on an atom in a compound, determined by electronegativity rules; tracks electron transfer in redox reactions"?

options.text: Oxidation state
pKa
Metallic bond
Heterogeneous catalyst

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Oxidation state".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which term is described by: "A ranking of metals by reactivity (tendency to lose electrons); a more active metal displaces a less active metal from solution"?

options.text: State function
Activity series
Heterogeneous catalyst
Metallic bond

quiz_questions.explanation: This description refers to "Activity series".

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following statements about Types of Chemical Reactions is accurate?

options.text: Comparing Qsp to Ksp predicts precipitation: if Qsp > Ksp, a precipitate forms.
The relative strength of an acid is inversely related to the strength of its conjugate base.
Strong acids (HCl, HBr, HI, H₂SO₄, HNO₃, HClO₄) and strong bases (group 1/2 hydroxides) dissociate completely.

Types of Chemical Reactions are classified into several categories: synthesis (A + B → AB), decomposition (AB → A + B), single replacement (A + BC → AC + B), double replacement (AB + CD → AD + CB), and combustion (fuel + O₂ → CO₂ + H₂O).

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Types of Chemical Reactions as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which statement correctly describes an aspect of Types of Chemical Reactions?

options.text: Energy exists as kinetic energy (motion) and potential energy (position/composition).
For aA + bB → products, rate = k[A]ᵐ[B]ⁿ, where m and n are reaction orders (determined experimentally, NOT from coefficients).
Alloys (mixtures of metals) modify properties by introducing different-sized atoms.
Oxidation-reduction (redox) reactions involve electron transfer; oxidation is loss of electrons (OIL), reduction is gain (RIG).

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Types of Chemical Reactions as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which is true specifically about Types of Chemical Reactions?

options.text: Q (reaction quotient) compared to K predicts the direction a reaction will shift.
Stoichiometric calculations convert between grams, moles, and particles using molar mass and mole ratios from balanced equations.
The choice of method depends on the properties of the mixture components and the purity required.
The activity series predicts whether single replacement reactions will occur.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Types of Chemical Reactions as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which best explains a key idea of Types of Chemical Reactions?

options.text: Mass number (A) = protons + neutrons.
Equilibrium Constants quantify the position of equilibrium.

Types of Chemical Reactions are classified into several categories: synthesis (A + B → AB), decomposition (AB → A + B), single replacement (A + BC → AC + B), double replacement (AB + CD → AD + CB), and combustion (fuel + O₂ → CO₂ + H₂O).
Activation energy (Ea) is the energy barrier that must be overcome for a reaction to proceed.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Types of Chemical Reactions as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which accurately reflects the content on Types of Chemical Reactions?

options.text: Chemical Bonding connects atoms through ionic, covalent, and metallic bonds.
Oxidation-reduction (redox) reactions involve electron transfer; oxidation is loss of electrons (OIL), reduction is gain (RIG).
Equilibrium Constants quantify the position of equilibrium.
K > 1 favors products; K < 1 favors reactants.

quiz_questions.explanation: The correct statement describes Types of Chemical Reactions as covered in this lesson.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following is a real-world application of Types of Chemical Reactions?

options.text: Road salt (NaCl) lowering the freezing point of water to prevent ice formation on roads—effective down to about -9°C (ΔTf = iKfm, i = 2 for NaCl)
Combustion of hydrocarbons in engines: CH₄ + 2O₂ → CO₂ + 2H₂O releasing energy to power vehicles; incomplete combustion produces toxic CO
Haber process optimization (N₂ + 3H₂ ⇌ 2NH₃) using high pressure and moderate temperature to shift equilibrium toward ammonia production for fertilizers
Petroleum refining using fractional distillation to separate crude oil into fractions: gases, gasoline, kerosene, diesel, lubricating oils, and asphalt

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Types of Chemical Reactions.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which example demonstrates the practical relevance of Types of Chemical Reactions?

options.text: Corrosion of iron (rusting): 4Fe + 3O₂ + 6H₂O → 4Fe(OH)₃ → 2Fe₂O₃·3H₂O; prevented by galvanizing (Zn coating) or painting
Industrial chemical production (ammonia synthesis: N₂ + 3H₂ → 2NH₃) using stoichiometry to optimize raw material usage and minimize waste
Antacid tablet surface area experiments: crushed tablets dissolve faster than whole tablets because increased surface area increases reaction rate
Lithium-ion batteries exploiting Li's small atomic radius and low ionization energy for high energy density in lightweight portable electronics

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Types of Chemical Reactions.

quiz_questions.question_text: How is Types of Chemical Reactions applied in real-world contexts?

options.text: Displacement reactions in metallurgy: extracting copper by placing iron in CuSO₄ solution (Fe + CuSO₄ → FeSO₄ + Cu, since Fe is more active than Cu)
Lithium-ion batteries exploiting Li's small atomic radius and low ionization energy for high energy density in lightweight portable electronics
Supercritical CO₂ used as a green solvent in decaffeination of coffee and dry cleaning, replacing toxic organic solvents
Antacid tablet surface area experiments: crushed tablets dissolve faster than whole tablets because increased surface area increases reaction rate

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Types of Chemical Reactions.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which scenario illustrates the principles of Types of Chemical Reactions?

options.text: Combustion of hydrocarbons in engines: CH₄ + 2O₂ → CO₂ + 2H₂O releasing energy to power vehicles; incomplete combustion produces toxic CO
Petroleum refining using fractional distillation to separate crude oil into fractions: gases, gasoline, kerosene, diesel, lubricating oils, and asphalt
Blood buffering: CO₂ + H₂O ⇌ H₂CO₃ ⇌ HCO₃⁻ + H⁺ with specific K values maintaining blood pH in the narrow range of 7.35-7.45
Stoichiometric calculations in pharmaceutical manufacturing ensuring precise drug composition, maintaining consistent dosage and efficacy

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Types of Chemical Reactions.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which best shows Types of Chemical Reactions in practice?

options.text: Lithium-ion batteries: rechargeable cells using Li⁺ intercalation in graphite (anode) and metal oxide (cathode); powering smartphones, laptops, and electric vehicles
Quality control in fuel production: measuring heat of combustion per gram to verify fuel grade and energy content
Corrosion of iron (rusting): 4Fe + 3O₂ + 6H₂O → 4Fe(OH)₃ → 2Fe₂O₃·3H₂O; prevented by galvanizing (Zn coating) or painting
Carbon dioxide equilibrium in oceans (CO₂ + H₂O ⇌ H₂CO₃ ⇌ HCO₃⁻ + H⁺) affected by rising atmospheric CO₂, causing ocean acidification

quiz_questions.explanation: This is a real-world application of Types of Chemical Reactions.
